message:
demo(time-travel): add nondeterministic failure recording scenario (bd-77g6j.1)

Seed-sweep demo that finds and records a cancel/obligation race condition
to an .ftrace file. Creates 8 tasks with obligations across two regions,
cancels one region, and detects obligation leaks where a "late" obligation
is not resolved before the region closes (~1/500 probability per seed).

Deliverables: examples/demo_record_nondeterministic.rs (test program source),
Makefile with `make demo-record` target, .ftrace output via DEMO_TRACE_DIR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2wrsc","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] CALM Analysis for Saga Coordination Points","description":"The CALM theorem (§13.3) proves that monotone programs can be implemented without coordination (consistency without locks). Asupersync's saga system currently coordinates at every step, but many saga operations are monotone and could execute coordination-free. Applying CALM analysis would: (1) Identify which saga operations are monotone (can run without coordination), (2) Minimize synchronization barriers to only non-monotone operations, (3) Reduce latency by eliminating unnecessary coordination. This is a Tier 2 priority because it directly improves saga performance. Implementation: Annotate saga operations with monotonicity markers. Use static analysis to verify monotonicity claims. Generate optimized saga execution plans that skip coordination for monotone steps. Reference: Hellerstein & Alvaro, 'Keeping CALM: When Distributed Consistency is Easy' (2020).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:16:08.203673619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:14.494396004Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","calm","sagas"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2wrsc","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2wrsc.1","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Identify monotone vs non-monotone saga operations","description":"Audit all existing saga operations in Asupersync and classify each as monotone or non-monotone per the CALM theorem (Hellerstein and Alvaro 2020, Graveyard section 13.3). Classification criteria: Monotone = only adds information (inserts, appends, set unions, lattice joins, counter increments). Non-monotone = depends on negation or absence (deletes, reads-before-writes, threshold checks, aggregations over incomplete sets). Deliverable: Classification table in docs/calm_analysis.md. Methodology: (1) Enumerate all saga protocols, (2) List every step and side effects, (3) Classify using lattice test, (4) Identify minimum coordination points for non-monotone steps, (5) Compute critical path. Acceptance: Every saga operation classified, at least 30% monotone. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: (Analysis deliverable -- define logging contract for runtime monotonicity checks.) INFO: CALM classification complete: total_steps={n}, monotone={m}, non_monotone={nm}, ratio={ratio:.2f}. DEBUG: step classified: saga={saga_name}, step={step_name}, classification={mono|non_mono}, justification={reason}. WARN: ambiguous classification: saga={saga_name}, step={step_name} -- step appears monotone in isolation but non-monotone in composition. Needs manual review. Metrics (runtime, for future use): saga_step_monotonicity (gauge by saga and step, 1=monotone 0=non-monotone), saga_monotone_ratio (gauge by saga), calm_coordination_barriers_total (counter by saga).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:16:14.300951691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:04:10.396418391Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T00:04:10.396266439Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","analysis","calm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2wrsc.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2wrsc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2wrsc.2","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Implement coordination-free execution for monotone saga steps","description":"For saga operations classified as monotone (per the CALM theorem analysis in bd-2wrsc.1), implement a coordination-free execution path that bypasses synchronization barriers. Instead of waiting for confirmation at each step, monotone steps execute speculatively and merge results via lattice join.

BACKGROUND (CALM Theorem, Graveyard section 13.3): The CALM theorem (Hellerstein and Alvaro, Keeping CALM: When Distributed Consistency is Easy, 2020) proves that monotone programs -- those whose outputs grow monotonically with inputs -- can execute without coordination while preserving consistency. Non-monotone operations (deletes, threshold checks, negation) require coordination because new input can invalidate previous conclusions.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Define a Lattice<T> trait with join, bottom, laws: associativity, commutativity, idempotency. Use lattices or crdts crate as reference. (2) Implement MonotoneSagaExecutor that groups consecutive monotone steps into coordination-free batches, executes concurrently via tokio::JoinSet, merges outputs via Lattice::join, inserts coordination barriers only before non-monotone steps. (3) Data structures: SagaPlan with Vec<SagaStep> where SagaStep has monotonicity field. SagaExecutionPlan with batches that are either CoordinationFree(Vec<SagaStep>) or Coordinated(SagaStep). (4) Conflict detection at boundaries: before executing a non-monotone step, verify all preceding monotone outputs have merged to fixed point. (5) Fallback: if monotonicity annotation is incorrect (post-hoc verification), abort coordination-free execution and fall back to fully-coordinated. Log fallback reason to EvidenceLedger.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) Sagas with 100% monotone steps execute with zero coordination barriers. (2) Mixed sagas use coordination only at non-monotone boundaries. (3) Lattice merge produces identical final state regardless of execution order (1000 random orderings via FrankenLab). (4) Fallback triggers correctly when monotonicity is violated. (5) Latency improvement >= 1.5x for sagas with >50% monotone steps (criterion.rs, 95% CI excludes 1.0).

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Structured JSON via tracing with fields: saga_id, batch_index, batch_type, step_count, merge_count, merge_latency_us, final_state_hash. EvidenceLedger entry per saga execution: component=saga_executor, action=calm_optimized|fully_coordinated, top_features includes monotone_step_ratio. On fallback: WARN log with saga_id, failed_step_id, violation_reason. Trace span saga::batch with otel attributes.

TESTING APPROACH: Unit tests verify Lattice trait laws (proptest 10000 cases). Integration tests run mixed sagas under FrankenLab deterministic scheduler. Benchmark with criterion.rs for CALM-optimized vs baseline.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:16:20.154381487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:18:53.914723131Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T00:18:53.914594402Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","calm","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2wrsc.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2wrsc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2wrsc.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2wrsc.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2wrsc.2","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2wrsc.3","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Unit and E2E tests for CALM-optimized saga execution","description":"Comprehensive test suite for CALM-based saga optimization. Tests must verify that the monotone/non-monotone classification is correct and that coordination-free execution preserves saga semantics.

**Unit tests:**
(1) Monotonicity classifier correctness — for each saga operation, verify the classification against known ground truth (manually verified monotone/non-monotone labels).
(2) Lattice merge semantics — verify that lattice join of concurrent monotone outputs produces the correct merged result (associativity, commutativity, idempotency).
(3) Conflict detection at non-monotone boundaries — verify that the system correctly detects when a non-monotone operation follows monotone speculation and triggers coordination.

**E2E tests:**
(4) End-to-end saga with mixed monotone/non-monotone steps — run 1000 random orderings and verify that all produce identical final state (deterministic replay via FrankenLab).
(5) Performance comparison — measure latency of CALM-optimized sagas vs fully-coordinated baselines; assert >= 1.5x improvement for sagas with >50% monotone steps.
(6) Fault injection — inject partitions and crashes during coordination-free monotone phases; verify that the saga still converges to correct final state after recovery.

**Logging requirements:**
- Each test must log: saga_id, step_id, monotonicity_classification, coordination_skipped (bool), lattice_merge_count, final_state_hash.
- All logs written to EvidenceLedger in JSONL format for offline replay.
- On failure, emit a full saga execution trace with all scheduling decisions.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- 100% of monotonicity classifications match manual ground truth.
- 0 semantic divergences across 1000 random orderings.
- Latency improvement measurable with 95% confidence interval excluding 1.0.
- All fault injection scenarios converge to correct state.

**Rust implementation notes:**
- Use proptest for property-based testing of lattice laws.
- Use FrankenLab deterministic scheduler for replay tests.
- Use criterion.rs for performance comparison benchmarks.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:01.307178041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:33.717593656Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","calm","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2wrsc.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2wrsc.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2wrsc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2wrsc.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2wrsc.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2wrsc.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2wrsc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2x41","title":"[ALIEN][RAPTORQ][PROOF] Artifact replay/verification helper","description":"## Purpose

- Useful error output when replay diverges.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:16:07.491740622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:23:31.150934425Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:23:31.150850649Z","close_reason":"Implemented DecodeProof::replay_and_verify + detailed mismatch errors and tests in src/raptorq/proof.rs","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","raptorq","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x41","depends_on_id":"bd-20to","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:16Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2x5xc","title":"[SPORK][API] Unified Error Taxonomy (App/Supervisor/GenServer/Registry)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacHollow","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:47:22.289462874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T11:37:47.872312418Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T11:37:47.872162410Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","errors","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x5xc","depends_on_id":"bd-11z9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:15Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x5xc","depends_on_id":"bd-1f3nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:15Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":975,"issue_id":"bd-2x5xc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Unify error surfaces so callers don't need to memorize 10 enums.

Design:
- A small set of domain errors:
  - start/stop/join errors
  - call/cast errors
  - registry lease errors
  - supervision errors

## Acceptance

- Error mapping is deterministic and monotone in severity (align `bd-2p4vf`).
- No silent drops: every failure is represented.
","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:47:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xaly","title":"Fix eprocess config validation: max_evalue bounds + p0 constraints","description":"EProcessConfig::validate() missing: (1) max_evalue must be >0, finite, >=1.0; (2) max_evalue >= 1.0/alpha check; (3) p0 >= 0.5 allows negative betting factors. Found during code audit.","notes":"Fixed in 091daa4: corrected lambda bounds, added max_evalue validation, 3 new tests, all 47 eprocess tests pass","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:15:59.396534970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:23:10.179282871Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T04:23:10.179116973Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@
+# Asupersync convenience targets.
+#
+# This Makefile wraps common cargo commands and demo workflows.
+# All heavy lifting is done by Cargo; these are shortcuts.
+
+.PHONY: test check clippy fmt demo-record clean
+
+# Default: run full test suite.
+test:
+	cargo test --all-features
+
+# Type-check without codegen.
+check:
+	cargo check --all-features
+
+# Lint check (deny warnings).
+clippy:
+	cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings
+
+# Format check.
+fmt:
+	cargo fmt -- --check
+
+# Record a nondeterministic failure demo trace.
+#
+# Sweeps seeds to find a cancel/obligation race condition, then records the
+# failing execution to an .ftrace file for deterministic replay.
+#
+# Environment variables:
+#   DEMO_SEED_START  - First seed to try (default: 0)
+#   DEMO_SEED_COUNT  - Number of seeds to sweep (default: 10_000)
+#   DEMO_TRACE_DIR   - Output directory for .ftrace files (default: .)
+demo-record:
+	cargo run --example demo_record_nondeterministic --features test-internals
+
+clean:
+	cargo clean
